cards.title: Having children makes you smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics from parenting appear to boost cognitive health.

cards.title: Managing anxiety on family holidays
cards.body: Excessive worry can undermine family vacations, but learning to cope during real emergencies builds confidence. A parent discovered she could handle crises better than she expected.

cards.title: How childhood shapes adult relationships
cards.body: Difficult childhoods with narcissistic or avoidant parents can impact adult relationship patterns. Learning what healthy relationships look like takes time and self-awareness.

cards.title: Understanding kids' attention-seeking behavior
cards.body: Children demand attention for developmental reasons, not manipulation. Stricter boundaries rarely work; understanding the underlying need is more effective.

cards.title: Teaching kids decision-making skills
cards.body: Brains don't fully develop until age 25, so teens need guidance with major decisions. Parents can help by supporting good choices during this critical period.

cards.title: Verbal and emotional abuse from parents
cards.body: Adult children experiencing ongoing parental verbal abuse often doubt themselves and feel anxious. Recognizing these patterns is the first step to reclaiming your voice.

cards.title: Home schooling while pursuing tennis
cards.body: Hunter Henderson, 10, balances competitive tennis with home schooling while traveling. The young Doncaster player recently won a major international tournament.

cards.title: Maternity care failings investigated
cards.body: An independent inquiry found unacceptable racism and discrimination in maternity services affecting patient safety. The government has promised action on these systemic failures.
